title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. turcestanica
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. turcestanica, also known as Turkestan orchid or pink helleborine, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Dactylorhiza incarnata species, a type of terrestrial orchid native to Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Turkestan orchid include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make salves and creams for topical application.
